summary: Typosquat of `codebuff` fetching unverified binary from unrelated personal GitHub account
details: The package `codebuff-cli` ships a verbatim copy of the official Codebuff README (which directs users to `npm install -g codebuff` and `github.com/CodebuffAI/codebuff/issues`), impersonating the legitimate CodebuffAI publisher. However, the bin wrapper at `cli/bin/codebuff.cjs` (line 10) hardcodes `REPO = 'Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli'` — an unrelated personal GitHub account. On first invocation of the `codebuff` command, the wrapper queries `api.github.com/repos/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/latest`, downloads `codebuff-<platform>-<arch>` into `~/.codebuff/bin/`, chmods it 0755, and spawns it. The URL is mutable (always `latest`, never version-pinned), no hash or signature verification is performed, and the binary's publisher does not match the package's claimed publisher. A second downloader at `cli/scripts/download-binary.cjs` duplicates the same logic. Whoever controls `Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli` can ship arbitrary native code to every developer who installs this package expecting the official Codebuff CLI, at any time, simply by publishing a new GitHub release.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
